assembly_instructions: One-off experimental direct-drive steam-turbine locomotive from 1944 (PRR #6200). 6-8-6 wheel arrangement: three-axle leading truck under the smokebox, four coupled driving axles under the boiler (68-inch wheels), three-axle trailing truck under the Belpaire firebox. A long cylindrical boiler links the cylindrical smokebox at the front to the firebox at the rear; a Worthington feedwater heater rides on top of the boiler, a steam dome sits amidships, and the iconic quadruple exhaust stack crowns the smokebox. Two large 'elephant-ear' smoke deflectors flank the smokebox. The direct-drive turbine housing and reduction gearbox sit under the cab, driving wheel #4 directly and the rest via the coupling side rods. A 16-wheel 180-P-85 tender (two 4-axle trucks) trails behind. Scale 0.5× real — ≈18.7 m long.

info.japanese_name: ペンシルバニア鉄道 S2形 蒸気タービン機関車 (1944)
info.english_name: Pennsylvania Railroad class S2 steam-turbine locomotive
info.summary: One-off 1944 experimental 6-8-6 direct-drive steam-turbine locomotive (PRR #6200). The only locomotive ever built with a 6-8-6 wheel arrangement.
info.description: The Pennsylvania Railroad's S2 (#6200) was a one-off experimental steam-turbine locomotive built by Baldwin in 1944 to see whether marine-style direct-drive turbines could keep steam competitive against emerging diesels. It is the only locomotive ever built with a 6-8-6 wheel arrangement — a three-axle leading truck, four driven axles (68-inch driving wheels), and a three-axle trailing truck under a massive Belpaire firebox. Rather than reciprocating pistons, the S2 used a Westinghouse ahead-turbine (with a smaller reverse turbine) geared through a reduction transmission to one driving axle; the remaining driving axles were connected via conventional side rods. The engine incorporated a Worthington feedwater heater, a quadruple exhaust stack to give the turbine enough draft, and distinctive 'elephant-ear' smoke deflectors (added December 1946 at Altoona) to lift the plume over the unusually long boiler. A 180-P-85 tender with sixteen wheels on two 4-axle trucks carried 19,500 US gal of water and 37.5 short tons of coal. The S2 ran passenger service in 1944–1949 but suffered from turbine blade wear and poor efficiency at low speeds, and was scrapped in 1952.
info.status: Retired / scrapped; Wikipedia docs CC BY-SA 4.0

facts.label: Wheel arrangement
facts.value: 6-8-6 — the only locomotive ever built with this layout

facts.label: Signature details
facts.value: Quadruple stack, Worthington feedwater heater, 'elephant-ear' smoke deflectors

parts.name: Pilot / Cowcatcher
parts.role: Wedge-shaped front pilot at the very front of the engine.

parts.name: Smokebox
parts.role: Short cylindrical chamber at the front of the boiler. Collects exhaust gas and channels it up the stack.

parts.name: Modified Belpaire Boiler
parts.role: Main pressure vessel — 102 in (2.59 m) diameter, 310 psi. Generates the steam that feeds the turbine.

parts.name: Belpaire Firebox
parts.role: Belpaire firebox at the rear of the boiler. 120 sq ft grate area.

parts.name: Cab
parts.role: Crew compartment over the trailing truck. Houses the single-lever fluid-pressure control.

parts.name: Headlight
parts.role: Front-facing headlight centered on the smokebox door.

parts.name: Locomotive Bell
parts.role: Brass warning bell mounted on top of the smokebox.

parts.name: Quadruple Exhaust Stack
parts.role: Signature four-tube exhaust stack — gave the turbine exhaust enough draft pull despite the long boiler.

parts.name: Steam Dome
parts.role: Brass-capped dome on top of the boiler — collects dry steam from the steam space before it reaches the turbine.

parts.name: Worthington Feedwater Heater
parts.role: Worthington Corporation feedwater heater — pre-heats boiler feedwater using exhaust steam for efficiency.

parts.name: Smoke Deflector ('elephant ear', Port)
parts.role: Large vertical deflector plate along the port side of the smokebox. Added Dec 1946 at Altoona.

parts.name: Smoke Deflector ('elephant ear', Stbd)
parts.role: Starboard-side smoke deflector.

parts.name: Running Board (Port)
parts.role: Long port-side running board for maintenance access along the boiler.

parts.name: Twin Air Pump
parts.role: One of the twin air pumps beneath the running boards — supplies compressed air for the train brakes.

parts.name: Direct-Drive Steam Turbine
parts.role: Westinghouse ahead-turbine (with smaller reverse-turbine pair) under the cab. Feeds the reduction gearbox.

parts.name: Reduction Transmission
parts.role: Reduction gearbox between the turbine shaft and the driven axle.

parts.name: Leading Truck Frame (3-axle)
parts.role: Three-axle (six-wheel) leading truck frame under the smokebox.

parts.name: Leading Wheel (front axle, port)
parts.role: Port-side wheel on the front leading axle (two more axles visible as part of the truck frame).

parts.name: Leading Wheel (rear axle, port)
parts.role: Port-side wheel on the rearmost leading axle.

parts.name: Driving Wheel #1 (68 in)
parts.role: Rearmost driving wheel (port side) — 68-inch diameter, linked to the others via side rods.

parts.name: Driving Wheel #2
parts.role: Second driving wheel.

parts.name: Driving Wheel #3
parts.role: Third driving wheel.

parts.name: Driving Wheel #4 (geared axle)
parts.role: Frontmost driving wheel — sits on the directly-geared axle fed by the reduction transmission.

parts.name: Connecting Side Rod (Port)
parts.role: Coupling rod that ties all four driving axles together on the port side — so torque from the geared axle distributes across all eight driving wheels.

parts.name: Trailing Truck Frame (3-axle)
parts.role: Three-axle (six-wheel) trailing truck under the firebox.

parts.name: Trailing Wheel (front axle, port)
parts.role: Port-side wheel on the frontmost trailing axle.

parts.name: Trailing Wheel (rear axle, port)
parts.role: Port-side wheel on the rearmost trailing axle.

parts.name: 180-P-85 Tender Body
parts.role: 16-wheel tender carrying 19,500 gal of water and 37.5 short tons of coal.

parts.name: Tender Truck (Front, 4-axle)
parts.role: Forward tender truck — four axles, eight wheels.

parts.name: Tender Truck (Rear, 4-axle)
parts.role: Rear tender truck — four axles, eight wheels.

parts.name: Tender Wheel (Fwd Truck, axle A)
parts.role: Representative port wheel on the forward tender truck (front axle).

parts.name: Tender Wheel (Fwd Truck, axle B)
parts.role: Representative port wheel on the forward tender truck (rear axle).

parts.name: Tender Wheel (Rear Truck, axle A)
parts.role: Representative port wheel on the rear tender truck (front axle).

parts.name: Tender Wheel (Rear Truck, axle B)
parts.role: Representative port wheel on the rear tender truck (rear axle).
